name_policy: Personal names remain in original English.
spoiler_policy: Only information available through each current paragraph is used.

blocks.original_text: My lady, you ought cover your head,” Ser Rodrik told her as their horses plodded north. “You will take a chill.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik催Catelyn遮住头，免得雨中受寒。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “It is only water, Ser Rodrik,” Catelyn replied. Her hair hung wet and heavy, a loose strand stuck to her forehead, and she could imagine how ragged and wild she must look, but for once she did not care. The southern rain was soft and warm. Catelyn liked the feel of it on her face, gentle as a mother’s kisses. It took her back to her childhood, to long grey days at Riverrun. She remembered the godswood, drooping branches heavy with moisture, and the sound of her brother’s laughter as he chased her through piles of damp leaves. She remembered making mud pies with Lysa, the weight of them, the mud slick and brown between her fingers. They had served them to Littlefinger, giggling, and he’d eaten so much mud he was sick for a week. How young they all had been.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn享受温暖的南方雨，回忆Riverrun童年与Lysa、Littlefinger一起玩泥巴。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 温暖的雨触发童年记忆，使返乡路线不只是地图选择，也是Catelyn旧身份的回潮；Littlefinger已被嵌进她最早的家庭记忆。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn had almost forgotten. In the north, the rain fell cold and hard, and sometimes at night it turned to ice. It was as likely to kill a crop as nurture it, and it sent grown men running for the nearest shelter. That was no rain for little girls to play in.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她对比北方冰冷坚硬、会毁坏庄稼的雨。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “I am soaked through,” Ser Rodrik complained. “Even my bones are wet.” The woods pressed close around them, and the steady pattering of rain on leaves was accompanied by the small sucking sounds their horses made as their hooves pulled free of the mud. “We will want a fire tonight, my lady, and a hot meal would serve us both.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 浑身湿透的Ser Rodrik只想要火、热餐和干衣服。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “There is an inn at the crossroads up ahead,” Catelyn told him. She had slept many a night there in her youth, traveling with her father. Lord Hoster Tully had been a restless man in his prime, always riding somewhere. She still remembered the innkeep, a fat woman named Masha Heddle who chewed sourleaf night and day and seemed to have an endless supply of smiles and sweet cakes for the children. The sweet cakes had been soaked with honey, rich and heavy on the tongue, but how Catelyn had dreaded those smiles. The sourleaf had stained Masha’s teeth a dark red, and made her smile a bloody horror.
blocks.explanation_zh: 前方crossroads inn唤起Masha Heddle红牙与童年点心的记忆。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Masha与旅店同时承担地标和记忆容器功能：路没有变，熟悉的人却已衰老、冷淡。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “An inn,” Ser Rodrik repeated wistfully. “If only . . . but we dare not risk it. If we wish to remain unknown, I think it best we seek out some small holdfast . . . ” He broke off as they heard sounds up the road; splashing water, the clink of mail, a horse’s whinny. “Riders,” he warned, his hand dropping to the hilt of his sword. Even on the kingsroad, it never hurt to be wary.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn担心旅店会暴露身份，忽然听见后方骑兵。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: They followed the sounds around a lazy bend of the road and saw them; a column of armed men noisily fording a swollen stream. Catelyn reined up to let them pass. The banner in the hand of the foremost rider hung sodden and limp, but the guardsmen wore indigo cloaks and on their shoulders flew the silver eagle of Seagard. “Mallisters,” Ser Rodrik whispered to her, as if she had not known. “My lady, best pull up your hood.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mallister骑士涉水而来，Ser Rodrik提醒她遮脸；鹰纹使她认出Seagard人。
blocks.literary_note_zh: heraldry在本章不是装饰，而是即时身份数据库；Catelyn能凭鹰纹、黑蝙蝠、红马辨认政治网络。
blocks.background_zh: **House Mallister：** Seagard的riverlord家族，以银鹰为纹章，效忠House Tully；Jason与Patrek正南下参加Hand’s tourney。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn made no move. Lord Jason Mallister himself rode with them, surrounded by his knights, his son Patrek by his side and their squires close behind. They were riding for King’s Landing and the Hand’s tourney, she knew. For the past week, the travelers had been thick as flies upon the kingsroad; knights and freeriders, singers with their harps and drums, heavy wagons laden with hops or corn or casks of honey, traders and craftsmen and whores, and all of them moving south.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn反而放下hood，看见Jason Mallister与儿子Patrek南下参加tourney。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: She studied Lord Jason boldly. The last time she had seen him he had been jesting with her uncle at her wedding feast; the Mallisters stood bannermen to the Tullys, and his gifts had been lavish. His brown hair was salted with white now, his face chiseled gaunt by time, yet the years had not touched his pride. He rode like a man who feared nothing. Catelyn envied him that; she had come to fear so much. As the riders passed, Lord Jason nodded a curt greeting, but it was only a high lord’s courtesy to strangers chance met on the road. There was no recognition in those fierce eyes, and his son did not even waste a look.
blocks.explanation_zh: Jason是父亲的封臣却未认出她；她短暂羡慕他们能公开骑行、无须隐藏。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 未被封臣认出既证明伪装成功，也制造身份疏离：她羡慕的不是Jason本人，而是公开行动的自由。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “He did not know you,” Ser Rodrik said after, wondering.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik惊讶竟无人认出他们。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “He saw a pair of mud-spattered travelers by the side of the road, wet and tired. It would never occur to him to suspect that one of them was the daughter of his liege lord. I think we shall be safe enough at the inn, Ser Rodrik.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn判断满身泥水的旅人不会被当成liege lord之女，因此决定住店。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn利用阶级想象隐藏身份：旁人不相信liege lord之女会如此狼狈，因此泥水反而成为伪装。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: It was near dark when they reached it, at the crossroads north of the great confluence of the Trident. Masha Heddle was fatter and greyer than Catelyn remembered, still chewing her sourleaf, but she gave them only the most cursory of looks, with nary a hint of her ghastly red smile. “Two rooms at the top of the stair, that’s all there is,” she said, chewing all the while. “They’re under the bell tower, you won’t be missing meals, though there’s some thinks it too noisy. Can’t be helped. We’re full up, or near as makes no matter. It’s those rooms or the road.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 他们抵达旅店；衰老的Masha只剩两间garret rooms，楼下又已客满。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: It was those rooms, low, dusty garrets at the top of a cramped narrow staircase. “Leave your boots down here,” Masha told them after she’d taken their coin. “The boy will clean them. I won’t have you tracking mud up my stairs. Mind the bell. Those who come late to meals don’t eat.” There were no smiles, and no mention of sweet cakes.
blocks.explanation_zh: Masha叫他们脱掉泥靴，并用钟声规定晚餐时间，昔日亲切已不复见。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: When the supper bell rang, the sound was deafening. Catelyn had changed into dry clothes. She sat by the window, watching rain run down the pane. The glass was milky and full of bubbles, and a wet dusk was falling outside. Catelyn could just make out the muddy crossing where the two great roads met.
blocks.explanation_zh: 晚餐钟响时，Catelyn隔着起泡玻璃望雨和道路，权衡下一步。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: The crossroads gave her pause. If they turned west from here, it was an easy ride down to Riverrun. Her father had always given her wise counsel when she needed it most, and she yearned to talk to him, to warn him of the gathering storm. If Winterfell needed to brace for war, how much more so Riverrun, so much closer to King’s Landing, with the power of Casterly Rock looming to the west like a shadow. If only her father had been stronger, she might have chanced it, but Hoster Tully had been bedridden these past two years, and Catelyn was loath to tax him now.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她可以向西去Riverrun求助病中的父亲，却担心靠近Casterly Rock会引发战争。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 去Riverrun最符合亲情，却在军事地理上最危险；她把个人求助可能引发的军队动员纳入判断。
blocks.background_zh: **Hoster Tully：** Catelyn的父亲、Lord of Riverrun；她已知他病重，但尚未亲见病情。Riverrun位于riverlands西部。

blocks.original_text: The eastern road was wilder and more dangerous, climbing through rocky foothills and thick forests into the Mountains of the Moon, past high passes and deep chasms to the Vale of Arryn and the stony Fingers beyond. Above the Vale, the Eyrie stood high and impregnable, its towers reaching for the sky. There she would find her sister . . . and, perhaps, some of the answers Ned sought. Surely Lysa knew more than she had dared to put in her letter. She might have the very proof that Ned needed to bring the Lannisters to ruin, and if it came to war, they would need the Arryns and the eastern lords who owed them service.
blocks.explanation_zh: 也可向东去Eyrie见Lysa，寻求答案与Arryn、Tully联盟。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eyrie提供答案和强大联盟，但“可能得到帮助”不等于“能安全抵达或说服Lysa”。
blocks.background_zh: **Eyrie与Lysa：** Lysa是Catelyn妹妹、Jon Arryn遗孀，现居难攻的Eyrie；姐妹关系并不自动保证立刻合作。

blocks.original_text: Yet the mountain road was perilous. Shadowcats prowled those passes, rock slides were common, and the mountain clans were lawless brigands, descending from the heights to rob and kill and melting away like snow whenever the knights rode out from the Vale in search of them. Even Jon Arryn, as great a lord as any the Eyrie had ever known, had always traveled in strength when he crossed the mountains. Catelyn’s only strength was one elderly knight, armored in loyalty.
blocks.explanation_zh: 但通往Eyrie的山路险恶，而她只有一名年老骑士护卫。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 一名忠诚的old knight能提供信任，却不能消除山路、brigands和人数差距带来的实际风险。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: No, she thought, Riverrun and the Eyrie would have to wait. Her path ran north to Winterfell, where her sons and her duty were waiting for her. As soon as they were safely past the Neck, she could declare herself to one of Ned’s bannermen, and send riders racing ahead with orders to mount a watch on the kingsroad.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她最终决定继续北上回到儿子身边，并计划过Neck后公开身份、召集北方封臣。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 北上决定重新把mother与Lady Stark身份合并：先回到sons身边，再以公开身份调动northmen。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: The rain obscured the fields beyond the crossroads, but Catelyn saw the land clear enough in her memory. The marketplace was just across the way, and the village a mile farther on, half a hundred white cottages surrounding a small stone sept. There would be more now; the summer had been long and peaceful. North of here the kingsroad ran along the Green Fork of the Trident, through fertile valleys and green woodlands, past thriving towns and stout holdfasts and the castles of the river lords.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她在心中复盘riverlands的道路与河流位置。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 南方暖雨唤起Riverrun童年；Catelyn在四方道路之间权衡亲情、安全、联盟与战争风险，最终决定继续北上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn knew them all: the Blackwoods and the Brackens, ever enemies, whose quarrels her father was obliged to settle; Lady Whent, last of her line, who dwelt with her ghosts in the cavernous vaults of Harrenhal; irascible Lord Frey, who had outlived seven wives and filled his twin castles with children, grandchildren, and great-grandchildren, and bastards and grandbastards as well. All of them were bannermen to the Tullys, their swords sworn to the service of Riverrun. Catelyn wondered if that would be enough, if it came to war. Her father was the staunchest man who’d ever lived, and she had no doubt that he would call his banners . . . but would the banners come? The Darrys and Rygers and Mootons had sworn oaths to Riverrun as well, yet they had fought with Rhaegar Targaryen on the Trident, while Lord Frey had arrived with his levies well after the battle was over, leaving some doubt as to which army he had planned to join (theirs, he had assured the victors solemnly in the aftermath, but ever after her father had called him the Late Lord Frey). It must not come to war, Catelyn thought fervently. They must not let it.
blocks.explanation_zh: 她盘点Blackwood、Bracken、Whent、Frey等家族的兵力与旧日立场，意识到任何召集都可能点燃战争。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 长名单体现她的政治素养，也揭示riverlands忠诚并非整齐统一；旧战争记忆会影响新危机。
blocks.background_zh: **riverlords：** Blackwood、Bracken、Whent与Frey等均处Tully体系内，但各家历史、邻里冲突和Robert’s Rebellion立场不同，忠诚不能机械等同。

blocks.original_text: Ser Rodrik came for her just as the bell ceased its clangor. “We had best make haste if we hope to eat tonight, my lady.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik催她下楼吃饭。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “It might be safer if we were not knight and lady until we pass the Neck,” she told him. “Common travelers attract less notice. A father and daughter taken to the road on some family business, say.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 他提议假扮父女，使两人关系显得自然。
blocks.literary_note_zh: father/daughter disguise把真实年龄差转化为可信社会关系，是现场表演而非单纯换衣。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “As you say, my lady,” Ser Rodrik agreed. It was only when she laughed that he realized what he’d done. “The old courtesies die hard, my—my daughter.” He tried to tug on his missing whiskers, and sighed with exasperation.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik习惯性称她my lady，暴露贵族礼仪难以彻底隐藏。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 一句my lady说明身体习惯和礼仪记忆比服装更难伪装；长期等级关系会从称呼中泄漏。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn took his arm. “Come, Father,” she said. “You’ll find that Masha Heddle sets a good table, I think, but try not to praise her. You truly don’t want to see her smile.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn接受安排，并提醒他别称赞Masha的笑容。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: The common room was long and drafty, with a row of huge wooden kegs at one end and a fireplace at the other. A serving boy ran back and forth with skewers of meat while Masha drew beer from the kegs, chewing her sourleaf all the while.
blocks.explanation_zh: 两人进入由壁炉和长桌组成的拥挤common room。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: The benches were crowded, townsfolk and farmers mingling freely with all manner of travelers. The crossroads made for odd companions; dyers with black and purple hands shared a bench with rivermen reeking of fish, an ironsmith thick with muscle squeezed in beside a wizened old septon, hard-bitten sellswords and soft plump merchants swapped news like boon companions.
blocks.explanation_zh: crossroads把不同方向、不同阶层的旅人临时混在同一屋檐下。
blocks.literary_note_zh: crossroads inn是叙事缩影：南北东西路线、不同家族与消息在此碰撞，所以偶遇既偶然又由地理促成。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: The company included more swords than Catelyn would have liked. Three by the fire wore the red stallion badge of the Brackens, and there was a large party in blue steel ringmail and capes of a silvery grey. On their shoulder was another familiar sigil, the twin towers of House Frey. She studied their faces, but they were all too young to have known her. The senior among them would have been no older than Bran when she went north.
blocks.explanation_zh: 屋中武器众多；Bracken与Frey随从人数可观，但都太年轻，不认识Catelyn。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn没有把年轻随从当作无关背景，而是立即评估数量、纹章、年龄与是否会认出自己。
blocks.background_zh: **旅店武装：** Bracken与Frey队伍是领主属下，不是专为Catelyn集结；她后来利用的是既有封臣关系。

blocks.original_text: Ser Rodrik found them an empty place on the bench near the kitchen. Across the table a handsome youth was fingering a woodharp. “Seven blessings to you, goodfolk,” he said as they sat. An empty wine cup stood on the table before him.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他们坐近厨房，一名年轻singer主动搭话。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “And to you, singer,” Catelyn returned. Ser Rodrik called for bread and meat and beer in a tone that meant now. The singer, a youth of some eighteen years, eyed them boldly and asked where they were going, and from whence they had come, and what news they had, letting the questions fly as quick as arrows and never pausing for an answer. “We left King’s Landing a fortnight ago,” Catelyn replied, answering the safest of his questions.
blocks.explanation_zh: singer连珠炮般打听消息；Catelyn只谨慎透露自己两周前离开King’s Landing。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她给Marillion的答案真实但有限，展示安全谎言未必需要虚构，只需控制信息量。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “That’s where I’m bound,” the youth said. As she had suspected, he was more interested in telling his own story than in hearing theirs. Singers loved nothing half so well as the sound of their own voices. “The Hand’s tourney means rich lords with fat purses. The last time I came away with more silver than I could carry . . . or would have, if I hadn’t lost it all betting on the Kingslayer to win the day.”
blocks.explanation_zh: singer要去tourney赚钱，并说自己上次押Jaime获胜而输掉银钱。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney的名声沿道路传播；Jaime和Loras的竞技结果已经成为赌局、歌曲与公共谈资。
blocks.background_zh: **Hand’s tourney：** King’s Landing即将举行的盛会已吸引各地骑士、singers和赌徒，也造成道路人口流动。

blocks.original_text: “The gods frown on the gambler,” Ser Rodrik said sternly. He was of the north, and shared the Stark views on tournaments.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik说诸神不赞成赌博。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “They frowned on me, for certain,” the singer said. “Your cruel gods and the Knight of Flowers altogether did me in.”
blocks.explanation_zh: singer反驳说真正使他输钱的是Loras。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “No doubt that was a lesson for you,” Ser Rodrik said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik问他是否吸取教训。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “It was. This time my coin will champion Ser Loras.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 他声称吸取了，这次将改押Loras。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 笑点在于Marillion把失败解释为押错人，而不是赌博本身；所谓lesson learned其实毫无改变。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Ser Rodrik tried to tug at whiskers that were not there, but before he could frame a rebuke the serving boy came scurrying up. He laid trenchers of bread before them and filled them with chunks of browned meat off a skewer, dripping with hot juice. Another skewer held tiny onions, fire peppers, and fat mushrooms. Ser Rodrik set to lustily as the lad ran back to fetch them beer.
blocks.explanation_zh: 食物送到，Ser Rodrik专心进食。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “My name is Marillion,” the singer said, plucking a string on his woodharp. “Doubtless you’ve heard me play somewhere?”
blocks.explanation_zh: singer自称Marillion，并默认自己的名声理应人尽皆知。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: His manner made Catelyn smile. Few wandering singers ever ventured as far north as Winterfell, but she knew his like from her girlhood in Riverrun. “I fear not,” she told him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn坦言从未听过。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: He drew a plaintive chord from the woodharp. “That is your loss,” he said. “Who was the finest singer you’ve ever heard?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Marillion弹出哀怨和弦，反问谁才是最好的singer。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Alia of Braavos,” Ser Rodrik answered at once.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik答Alia of Braavos。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, I’m much better than that old stick,” Marillion said. “If you have the silver for a song, I’ll gladly show you.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Marillion夸口胜过Alia，愿为银币献唱。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “I might have a copper or two, but I’d sooner toss it down a well than pay for your howling,” Ser Rodrik groused. His opinion of singers was well known; music was a lovely thing for girls, but he could not comprehend why any healthy boy would fill his hand with a harp when he might have had a sword.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik说只愿把铜币扔井里，并认为音乐适合少女、不适合健康男孩。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Ser Rodrik对音乐的看法显示旧式武人把艺术性视为不够阳刚，Marillion则靠机锋反击年龄和身份。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Your grandfather has a sour nature,” Marillion said to Catelyn. “I meant to do you honor. An homage to your beauty. In truth, I was made to sing for kings and high lords.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Marillion反唇相讥，改称要以歌赞美Catelyn。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, I can see that,” Catelyn said. “Lord Tully is fond of song, I hear. No doubt you’ve been to Riverrun.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn以父亲Hoster爱歌为饵，试探Marillion是否真去过Riverrun。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn主动设置可核验细节，开始反向审问爱吹嘘的陌生人；她并非被动听众。
blocks.background_zh: **家庭关系：** Hoster是Catelyn与Edmure的父亲；Edmure留在riverlands，是House Tully继承人。

blocks.original_text: “A hundred times,” the singer said airily. “They keep a chamber for me, and the young lord is like a brother.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Marillion夸称去过百次、与Edmure情同手足，反而因夸张露出破绽。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Marillion过度声称与Edmure亲密，恰好触碰Catelyn掌握的一手事实，因此自我揭穿。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Catelyn smiled, wondering what Edmure would think of that. Another singer had once bedded a girl her brother fancied; he had hated the breed ever since. “And Winterfell?” she asked him. “Have you traveled north?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn知道Edmure厌恶singers，转问他是否到过Winterfell。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她与Ser Rodrik以父女身份进入旅店，在纹章、人数和闲谈中评估环境；Marillion的吹嘘构成短暂喜剧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Why would I?’ Marillion asked. “It’s all blizzards and bearskins up there, and the Starks know no music but the howling of wolves.” Distantly, she was aware of the door banging open at the far end of the room.
blocks.explanation_zh: Marillion以南方偏见描绘北方；此时门被推开。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 他对north的描述由传闻和偏见组成；门在此打开，真正来自north路线的人立刻打断幻想。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Innkeep,” a servant’s voice called out behind her, “we have horses that want stabling, and my lord of Lannister requires a room and a hot bath.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 仆人高声宣布一位Lannister lord要房间和热水澡。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Oh, gods,” Ser Rodrik said before Catelyn reached out to silence him, her fingers tightening hard around his forearm.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik脱口惊呼，Catelyn立刻抓住他的手臂制止。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Ser Rodrik的惊呼险些先暴露两人，Catelyn用身体动作而非语言制止，避免进一步吸引注意。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Masha Heddle was bowing and smiling her hideous red smile. “I’m sorry, m’lord, truly, we’re full up, every room.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Masha带着难看的笑解释客房已满。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: There were four of them, Catelyn saw. An old man in the black of the Night’s Watch, two servants . . . and him, standing there small and bold as life. “My men will steep in your stable, and as for myself, well, I do not require a large room, as you can plainly see.” He flashed a mocking grin. “So long as the fire’s warm and the straw reasonably free of fleas, I am a happy man.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion与Yoren及两名随从进门；Tyrion开玩笑说小房间、草堆也能接受。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion把不利处境转成笑话，保持对场面的语言控制；Yoren出现也把Wall路线与旅店相接。
blocks.background_zh: **北返队伍：** Tyrion刚从Wall南返，Yoren是Night’s Watch的wandering crow；另外两人为Tyrion随从。

blocks.original_text: Masha Heddle was beside herself. “M’lord, there’s nothing, it’s the tourney, there’s no help for it, oh . . . ”
blocks.explanation_zh: Masha惶恐重复没有房间。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion Lannister pulled a coin from his purse and flicked it up over his head, caught it, tossed it again. Even across the room, where Catelyn sat, the wink of gold was unmistakable.
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion抛出金币，以财富迅速改变资源分配。
blocks.literary_note_zh: gold使住宿从“没有”变为“有人愿让”，展示市场权力能立刻重排私人选择。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: A freerider in a faded blue cloak lurched to his feet. “You’re welcome to my room, m’lord.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 一名freerider立刻愿意让出房间。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Now there’s a clever man,” Lannister said as he sent the coin spinning across the room. The freerider snatched it from the air. “And a nimble one to boot.” The dwarf turned back to Masha Heddle. “You will be able to manage food, I trust?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion赞许并付款，又吩咐准备饭食。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Anything you like, m’lord, anything at all,” the innkeep promised. And may he choke on it, Catelyn thought, but it was Bran she saw choking, drowning on his own blood.
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn一度想亲手掐死他，却在想象中看见Bran窒息流血，暴露愤怒背后的母亲恐惧。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她想象Tyrion窒息却看见Bran受害，说明报复冲动无法带来纯粹满足，只会重新触发创伤。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Lannister glanced at the nearest tables. “My men will have whatever you’re serving these people. Double portions, we’ve had a long hard ride. I’ll take a roast fowl—chicken, duck, pigeon, it makes no matter. And send up a flagon of your best wine. Yoren, will you sup with me?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion要双份食物、烤鸡和酒，并邀请Yoren同桌。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Aye, m’lord, I will,” the black brother replied.
blocks.explanation_zh: Yoren答应。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: The dwarf had not so much as glanced toward the far end of the room, and Catelyn was thinking how grateful she was for the crowded benches between them when suddenly Marillion bounded to his feet. “My lord of Lannister!” he called out. “I would be pleased to entertain you while you eat. Let me sing you the lay of your father’s great victory at King’s Landing!”
blocks.explanation_zh: Marillion主动要唱Tywin胜利之歌讨好Tyrion。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Marillion先赞Loras、随后又准备唱Tywin，显示游唱者的赞美可随潜在赞助者迅速改向。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Nothing would be more likely to ruin my supper,” the dwarf said dryly. His mismatched eyes considered the singer briefly, started to move away . . . and found Catelyn. He looked at her for a moment, puzzled. She turned her face away, but too late. The dwarf was smiling. “Lady Stark, what an unexpected pleasure,” he said. “I was sorry to miss you at Winterfell.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion拒绝，却在人群中认出Catelyn并当众叫出Lady Stark。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion认出她的瞬间使伪装整体失效；他或许只是惊讶问候，却在公开空间造成不可逆的信息扩散。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Marillion gaped at her, confusion giving way to chagrin as Catelyn rose slowly to her feet. She heard Ser Rodrik curse. If only the man had lingered at the Wall, she thought, if only . . .
blocks.explanation_zh: Marillion震惊，Catelyn起身，Ser Rodrik低声咒骂；她懊恼Tyrion没有留在Wall。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Tyrion意外抵达，以gold解决住宿，却在拥挤common room中认出Catelyn，使伪装瞬间崩溃。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “Lady . . . Stark?” Masha Heddle said thickly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Masha也惊问她是否真是Lady Stark。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn转守为攻，依次借Whent、Bracken、Frey纹章确认封臣义务，公开指控并成功让众人拔剑拘捕Tyrion。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “I was still Catelyn Tully the last time I bedded here,” she told the innkeep. She could hear the muttering, feel the eyes upon her. Catelyn glanced around the room, at the faces of the knights and sworn swords, and took a deep breath to slow the frantic beating of her heart. Did she dare take the risk? There was no time to think it through, only the moment and the sound of her own voice ringing in her ears. “You in the corner,” she said to an older man she had not noticed until now. “Is that the black bat of Harrenhal I see embroidered on your surcoat, ser?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn不再隐藏，公开身份并根据黑蝙蝠纹章询问一名Whent家臣。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn没有逃跑或否认，而是把个人身份转化为政治资本：先让纹章持有者公开承认所属关系。
blocks.background_zh: **House Whent：** Harrenhal领主家族，纹章为黑蝙蝠；Catelyn借Lady Whent与Hoster的关系请求其men表态。

blocks.original_text: The man got to his feet. “It is, my lady.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 那人确认自己的归属。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn转守为攻，依次借Whent、Bracken、Frey纹章确认封臣义务，公开指控并成功让众人拔剑拘捕Tyrion。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “And is Lady Whent a true and honest friend to my father, Lord Hoster Tully of Riverrun?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn让他承认Lady Whent与Hoster Tully的友谊，把私人识别转化为封臣关系。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 她的问题设计只要求对方确认公认友谊，降低拒绝门槛，并让下一步求援看似履行既有义务。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “She is,” the man replied stoutly.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他再次确认。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn转守为攻，依次借Whent、Bracken、Frey纹章确认封臣义务，公开指控并成功让众人拔剑拘捕Tyrion。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Ser Rodrik rose quietly and loosened his sword in its scabbard. The dwarf was blinking at them, blank-faced, with puzzlement in his mismatched eyes.
blocks.explanation_zh: Ser Rodrik悄悄松剑出鞘，Tyrion仍困惑地观察。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn转守为攻，依次借Whent、Bracken、Frey纹章确认封臣义务，公开指控并成功让众人拔剑拘捕Tyrion。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “The red stallion was ever a welcome sight in Riverrun,” she said to the trio by the fire. “My father counts Jonos Bracken among his oldest and most loyal bannermen.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn再以红马纹章点名Bracken人，并询问Jonos是否忠于父亲。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 从Whent转到Bracken形成重复验证：她逐一把屋中陌生武装者重新分类为父亲封臣的men。
blocks.background_zh: **House Bracken：** Stone Hedge的riverlord家族，以红色stallion为纹章，Lord Jonos效忠Riverrun。

blocks.original_text: The three men-at-arms exchanged uncertain looks. “Our lord is honored by his trust,” one of them said hesitantly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Bracken men虽迟疑，仍表示会尊重这份忠诚。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn转守为攻，依次借Whent、Bracken、Frey纹章确认封臣义务，公开指控并成功让众人拔剑拘捕Tyrion。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: “I envy your father all these fine friends,” Lannister quipped, “but I do not quite see the purpose of this, Lady Stark.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tyrion以玩笑询问她究竟想做什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn转守为攻，依次借Whent、Bracken、Frey纹章确认封臣义务，公开指控并成功让众人拔剑拘捕Tyrion。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: She ignored him, turning to the large party in blue and grey. They were the heart of the matter; there were more than twenty of them. “I know your sigil as well: the twin towers of Frey. How fares your good lord, sers?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn转向人数最多的Frey队伍，问Walder近况。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Frey人数最多，因此最后点名既完成政治铺垫，也确保真正执行逮捕时有足够武力。
blocks.background_zh: **House Frey：** 控制Twins渡口的强大家族，名义上是Tully封臣；Walder Frey年老且子孙众多。

blocks.original_text: Their captain rose. “Lord Walder is well, my lady. He plans to take a new wife on his ninetieth name day, and has asked your lord father to honor the wedding with his presence.”
blocks.explanation_zh: 队长答Walder身体很好，九十岁仍准备结婚。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Catelyn转守为攻，依次借Whent、Bracken、Frey纹章确认封臣义务，公开指控并成功让众人拔剑拘捕Tyrion。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。

blocks.original_text: Tyrion Lannister sniggered. That was when Catelyn knew he was hers. “This man came a guest into my house, and there conspired to murder my son, a boy of seven,” she proclaimed to the room at large, pointing. Ser Rodrik moved to her side, his sword in hand. “In the name of King Robert and the good lords you serve, I call upon you to seize him and help me return him to Winterfell to await the king’s justice.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Catelyn借众人公开承认的封臣义务，指控Tyrion参与谋杀七岁儿子的阴谋，并以Robert和各领主之名要求逮捕他、押回Winterfell接受king’s justice。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 指控建立在Littlefinger对dagger归属的说法及Bran遇袭事实上；逮捕不等于证据成立。她成功调用feudal obligations，却也把秘密调查升级为公开冲突。
blocks.background_zh: **事实／推断：** Bran确曾遭刺客袭击，Catelyn也被Littlefinger告知dagger曾属Tyrion；但本章未提供独立核验。公开拘捕是政治与法律行动，不是罪名已经证明。

blocks.original_text: She did not know what was more satisfying: the sound of a dozen swords drawn as one or the look on Tyrion Lannister’s face.
blocks.explanation_zh: 十余把剑同时出鞘；Catelyn从刀剑声和Tyrion的表情判断自己的临场策略奏效。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 结尾以声音与表情确认权力逆转：Tyrion的gold刚支配旅店市场，Catelyn的name与oaths又支配持剑者。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在伪装、道路选择、纹章识别、旅店权力关系与Catelyn的临场判断。
